name: Hammerhand
text: As the Battle Mage chants an invocation, their fists become like hammers, falling upon their foes with great and terrible power.

name: Fireball
text: With a snap of the fingers, the Battle Mage summons forth a roiling ball of magical flame and hurls it at a nearby foe.

name: Curse of Arrow Attraction
text: With a cry and a flourish, the Battle Mage curses an enemy, causing missiles to seek them out with almost sentient awareness.

name: Pillar of Fire
text: The Battle Mage conjures a fireball which quickly grows to become a towering pillar. Loosed from their grasp, it advances towards the enemy.

name: Arcane Urgency
text: As the Battle Mage's allies march, time around them slows, allowing them to cover great distance in the blink of an eye.

name: Oaken Shield
text: The Battle Mage summons a mighty spectral oak around themselves and their allies, protecting them from the weapons of their enemies.

name: Curse of Cowardly Flight
text: As the war drums beat, the Battle Mage reaches into the minds of the enemy, tapping into their fears and weakening their intestinal fortitude.

name: The Summoning
text: From the Realm of Chaos, the Daemonologist summons forth daemonic beasts to strike at their foes.

name: Steed of Shadows
text: Coal-black daemonic steeds materialise from the air, bearing warriors aloft on wings of shadow.

name: Gathering Darkness
text: At the Daemonologist's command, strange forms gather about the foe, their whispered taunts draining the enemy's courage.

name: Daemonic Familiars
text: The Daemonologist's many familiars fall upon the foe, slashing and biting with incorporeal claws and teeth.

name: Daemonic Vessel
text: At the Daemonologist's bidding, otherworldly entities rush forth to take possession of their allies, filling their bodies with unnatural ferocity.

name: Vortex of Chaos
text: A swirling tear in reality opens, through which daemonic beasts grasp at those that venture too close.

name: Daemonic Vigour
text: As the Daemonologist mutters verses aloud from a proscribed text, their allies fill with a daemonic vitality.

name: Doombolt
text: At the Dark Mage's bidding, a bolt of black lightning tears from the heavens and stabs at the earth.

name: Word of Pain
text: The Dark Mage chants incomprehensible syllables, each more torturous than the last.

name: Stream of Corruption
text: From the fingertips of the Dark Mage corrupted magic flows, flaying the flesh of the enemy.

name: Infernal Gateway
text: The Dark Mage reveals a hidden passageway through the Realm of Chaos.

name: Phantasmagoria
text: Dark visions beset the enemy ranks, driving warriors into blind panic or suicidal rage.

name: Battle Lust
text: The builds within those near to the Dark Mage, filling them with a terrible lust for bloodshed.

name: Soul Eater
text: The Dark Mage reaches out a taloned hand to tear at the very soul of their enemy.

name: Storm Call
text: The Elementalist channels the fury of a storm towards their foes, causing strong winds and icy rains to batter against them.

name: Flaming Sword
text: With a simple gesture, the Elementalist summons a great, flaming sword with which to strike at their enemies.

name: Plague of Rust
text: As the Elementalist utters a curse, the armour of the enemy loses its lustre, corroding and crumbling from their bodies.

name: Summon Elemental Spirit
text: The Elementalist summons forth a powerful spirit of earth, wind, water or fire to do their bidding.

name: Earthen Ramparts
text: At the Elementalist's summoning, mighty earthen ramparts rise up about their allies.

name: Wind Blast
text: A mighty wind rips enemy warriors from their regiments, dashing them against the hard earth.

name: Travel Mystical Pathway
text: Tapping into the power of the Geomantic Web, the Elementalist opens a portal through which their allies can travel.

name: Drain Magic
text: As the High Mage draws the Winds of Magic to themselves, other Wizards find themselves struggling to access their powers.

name: Walk Between Worlds
text: The High Mage causes their allies to drift in and out of reality, enabling them to pass through terrain as if it was not there.

name: Fiery Convocation
text: At the High Mage's bidding, a roiling storm of fire erupts within the midst of the enemy line.

name: Tempest
text: The High Mage summons forth a tempest of rain and sleet, the centre of which is all but impenetrable.

name: Corporeal Unmaking
text: The physical forms of the High Mage's enemies begin to unravel, turning to dust on the wind.

name: Fury of Khaine
text: The High Mage beseeches Khaine, God of Murder, to bestow his fury on the High Mage's allies.

name: Shield of Saphery
text: White light bursts forth from the High Mage's outstretched hands, clinging to armour and arcing overhead to form a protective barrier around their allies.

name: Glittering Robe
text: Shimmering light radiates from the Illusionist's garb, dazzling and confounding the enemy.

name: Mind Razor
text: Torturous illusion grips the enemy. Only those with the strongest of wills can resist.

name: Shimmering Dragon
text: An incorporeal Dragon materialises over the battlefield, stooping low to bear aloft a mighty champion.

name: Column of Crystal
text: The Illusionist conjures forth an illusory column of crystal. All that see it believe it to be utterly impenetrable.

name: Confounding Convocation
text: The enemy is bombarded by wild and disorienting images and dancing lights that render them all but blind to the world around them.

name: Spectral Doppelganger
text: Like the surface of a mirror struck a mighty blow, the Illusionist's image fractures, revealing myriad doppelganger likenesses.

name: Miasmic Mirage
text: Around the feet of the enemy, the earth itself shifts and blurs, causing warriors to trip and stumble upon half-imagined obstacles.

name: The Dwellers Below
text: Beneath the enemy the cold dirt parts, revealing spectral hands that reach to grasp at the foe and pull them into the underworld.

name: Deathly Cabal
text: From beyond the veil of death, unquiet spirits gather protectively around the Necromancer, their presence causing the foe to recoil in fear and dread.

name: Unquiet Spirits
text: Evil spirits gather around the enemy, their spectral talons creeping beneath armour to tear at flesh.

name: Spiritual Vortex
text: The Necromancer gestures and dark energy swirls, opening a vortex into the realm of Morr, god of death.

name: Curse of Years
text: As dead things draw near, they feed hungrily upon the vital energy of the living, ageing them rapidly.

name: Spectral Steed
text: As the Necromancer chants words of dark power, a ghostly steed forms from the night air.

name: Spirit Leech
text: Chill winds gust and long shadows gather, sapping the enemy's will and sowing seeds of dread.

name: Fist of Gork (or Mork)
text: In response to the Shaman's frantic hopping and gesticulating, a great green fist materialises from the air and wallops the foe!

name: Vindictive Glare
text: Their brows furrowed and their teeth clenched, the Shaman's glare is so intense that their enemies visibly wither.

name: Hand of Mork (or Gork)
text: A huge green hand appears from above and, with great care, flicks a hero across the battlefield.

name: Bad Moon Rizin'
text: The Shaman summons a great pale moon above the battlefield. Trembling under its baleful gaze, the enemy can barely lift their weapons!

name: Evil Sun Shinin'
text: The sun is out and there's an enemy army over there – it's a great day for a battle! 'Ave at it Boyz!

name: 'Ere We Go!
text: The Shaman begins a simple chant which is quickly and enthusiastically taken up by their friends.

name: Foot of Gork (or Mork)
text: There's a massive green foot on the battlefield and it seems to be moving!

name: Tree Singing
text: The melodious song of the Wizard rouses the forest spirits, awakening them from their deep slumber. In moments, fresh growth bursts from the fertile earth, reaching rapidly towards the heavens above.

name: Forest Walker
text: At the Wizard's bidding a hidden gateway into the Eternal Realms reveals itself. Beyond this lies a series of vaulted chambers, each leading to a similar portal; some nearby, other many leagues away.

name: Flock of Doom (Wood Elves)
text: With a mighty bellow, the Wizard summons forth a great flock of hawks and shrikes to strike at their enemies and drive them from their forest realm.

name: Viletide
text: The Shaman calls to the creeping things that nest in the decaying undergrowth, creating a vile wave of spiders, centipedes and slugbeetles that swarm over the foe.

name: Devolve
text: Delving into their enemies' minds, the Shaman magnifies the savage and animalistic parts of their psyche until they are no more than growling beasts.

name: Mantle of Ghorok
text: Ghorok was a legendary Minotaur, ferocious as a storm. When possessed by his spirit-mantle, a Shaman becomes a terror on the battlefield, though to summon his rage is not without risk.

name: Winds of Chaos
text: A cold and vicious gale blows from the Northern Wastes, carrying with it the laughter of Dark Gods.

name: Acquiescence
text: The enemy is humbled by the beauty of Slaanesh, letting their weapons fall limply to their sides.

name: Fleshy Abundance
text: Great wobbling mounds of fat spill out from beneath the armour of the Sorcerer and their comrades.

name: Blue Fire
text: As the Sorcerer's hands draw strange runes in the air, their enemies are consumed by blue flames.

name: Plague Wind
text: Daemons of Nurgle only. Remains in Play. Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 12" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as dangerous terrain. The template scatters D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any unit (friend or foe, but not including Daemons of Nurgle) that moves through the template, or that the template moves over suffers a -1 modifier to its Toughness characteristic for the remainder of the turn.

name: Cacophonic Hymn
text: Daemons of Slaanesh only. Remains in Play. If this spell is cast, the effects of any enchantment spell previously cast on the target unit immediately expire. In addition, whilst this spell is in play, the target unit becomes subject to the Stupidity special rule.

name: Pink Fire
text: Daemons of Tzeentch only. The target enemy unit suffers D3+3 Strength 3 hits, each with an AP of - and with the Flaming Attacks special rule.

name: Gift of Mutation
text:
Daemons of Tzeentch only. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 8 or more, the target enemy unit suffers a -D3 modifier to one of the following characteristics (to a minimum of 1, chosen by the casting player). If this spell is cast with a casting result of 12 or more, the target enemy unit suffers a -D3 modifier to two of the following characteristics (to a minimum of 1, chosen by the casting player). This spell lasts until your next Start of Turn sub-phase:
Weapon SkillStrengthToughness

name: Brain Bursta
text: Channelling the violence of their peers, the Shaman projects it against their enemies in a brain-bursting wave.

name: Gaze of Gork
text: Gork gives the Shaman the ability to project beams of coruscating energy from their eyes, burning massive holes through everything they look at.

name: Curse of Hashut
text:
This spell can only target enemy characters. However, it may target any enemy character that is within range and that the caster can draw a line of sight to, regardless of the usual rules for targeting characters, and may even target an enemy character that has joined a unit or that is engaged in combat.
The target enemy character must immediately make a Toughness test. If this test is passed, it suffers D3 Strength 2 hits, each with an AP of -. If, however, this test is failed, it suffers D3+2 Strength 5 hits, with no armour or Regeneration saves permitted (Ward saves can be attempted as normal).

name: Storm of Ash
text: Until your next Start of Turn sub-phase, all enemy units suffer a -1 modifier to any rolls To Hit made whilst within 9" of the caster's model (rolls of a natural 6 are unaffected).

name: Flames of Hashut
text: A single enemy unit the caster is engaged in combat with suffers D3+1 Strength 4 hits, each with an AP of -1. These hits have the Flaming Attacks special rule.

name: Apotheosis
text:
This spell can only target friendly characters whose troop type is infantry or cavalry, but may target characters engaged in combat. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 8 or more, the target friendly model immediately regains D3 lost Wounds. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 10 or more, the target friendly model immediately regains D3+1 lost Wounds.
In addition, the target friendly character gains the Fear special rule. If they already have the Fear special rule, they instead gain the Terror special rule.

name: Monsoon
text:
Remains in Play. Place a large (5") blast template so that its central hole is within 15" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as dangerous terrain over which no line of sight can be drawn. The template scatters D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any enemy for there move though the template becomes 'drenched' for the remainder of the game.
A drenched unit suffers a -1 To Hit modifier during the Shooting phase. A drenched model whose troop type is 'war machine' must roll a D6 before shooting. On a roll of 1, the model fails to shoot this turn.

name: Mork’s Curse
text: The Shaman points a bony finger at the enemy and utters a curse. Almost immediately, buckles begin to break and rivets pop, causing armour plates to slip.

name: Itchy Nuisance
text: The Shaman begins vigorously scratching and cackling maniacally, their antics causing their enemies to feel uncomfortably hot and itchy.

name: Cursing Word
text: Remains in Play. Whilst this spell is in play, the target enemy unit suffers a -1 modifier to either its Weapon skill characteristic, or its Ballistic Skill characteristic (chosen by the casting Wizard’s controlling player, to minimum of 1). If this spell is cast, the effects of any other Hex previously cast on the target unit immediately expire.

name: Black Horror
text: Remains in Play. Place a large (5") blast template so that its central hole is within 18" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as difficult terrain. The template scatters 2D6" during every Start of Turn sub-phase. Any enemy unit that moves through the template, or that the template moves over must immediately make D6 Strength tests. For each test that is failed, the unit loses a single Wound.

name: Djaf's Incantation of Cursed Blades
text: As the Liche Priest utters this ancient mantra, the weapons of the Nehekharan warriors gathered about them become imbued with the essence of Djaf, the jackal-headed god of the dead, who hungers for the souls of the living above all things.

name: Khsar's Incantation of the Desert Wind
text: Harnessing the power of Khsar, god of the desert winds, the Liche Priest summons forth a sandstorm that engulfs the undying warriors of Nehekhara and carries them across the battlefield.

name: Usekhp's Incantation of Desiccation
text: As the Liche Priest intones the curse of desiccation every syllable strips the moisture from their victims' bodies, sapping their strength and vitality.

name: Primordial Gloom
text: As the Bray-Shaman bellows praise to the gods, the sky above darkens and the soil underfoot swarms with unclean creatures.

name: Fury of the Beast
text: Driven by the Bray-Shaman's invocations, the Children of Chaos flow like a tide of violence over the enemy.

name: Call of the Wild
text: Filled with primal energy, the Children of Chaos move across the battlefield with unwholesome vigour.

name: Strangleroot
text: The Bray-Shaman gestures towards the foe, summoning twisted and blackened roots to grasp and strangle its enemies.

name: In the Gloaming Wildwood
text: The words of half-forgotten folk tales fill the enemy's minds, instilling them with a childlike fear of the dark forests.

name: 'Neath The Shaden Wodespan
text: Spectral forests loom above the battlefield, spreading creeping shadows that fill the enemy with dread.

name: Flock of Doom (Beastmen)
text: Summoned by the Bray-Shaman's cries, a flock of carrion birds descends upon the foe, striking at eyes and tearing through flesh.

name: Hand of Khaine
text: With a simple gesture, white light bursts forth from the Mage's outstretched hand, banishing the life force of the foe.

name: Courage of Aenarion
text: The High Elf Mage draws upon the Winds of Magic to conjure glorious images of Aenarion in the minds of their allies. So inspired, already brave warriors are filled with the unbreakable courage of the first Phoenix King.

name: Vaul's Unmaking
text: Glowing swords grow dim, blood-warm chalices cool and enchanted scrolls crumble into dust, their magical energies drained as the Mage turns the fury of Vaul's forge upon the magical trappings of the enemy.

name: Toothcracker
text: If this spell is cast with a casting result of 7 or more, the target friendly unit gains a +1 modifier to its Toughness characteristic (to a maximum of 10). If this spell is cast with a casting result of 10 or more, the target friendly unit gains a +2 modifier to its Toughness characteristic (to a maximum of 10). This spell lasts until the end of this turn.

name: Trollguts
text: If this spell is cast with a casting result of 8 or more, the target friendly unit gains the Regeneration (6+) special rule. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 11 or more, the target friendly unit gains the Regeneration (5+) special rule. This spell lasts until your next Start of Turn sub-phase.

name: Skitterleap
text:
This spell can only target friendly characters whose Troop Type is infantry, but may target characters engaged in combat. You may immediately remove the target friendly character from the battlefield and replace it anywhere completely within 24" of its original location, but not within 6" of any enemy models. The target cannot move again during this Movement phase.
Note that this spell allows a character to leave combat.

name: Warp Lightning
text: The target enemy unit suffers D3+1 Strength 5 hits, each with an AP of -3.

name: Cloud of Corruption
text: Remains in Play. Place a small (3") blast template so that its central hole is within 9" of the caster. Whilst in play, the template is treated as dangerous terrain over which no line of sight can be drawn. Whilst within 3" of one or more of these templates, enemy units suffer a -1 modifier to their Toughness characteristic (to a minimum of one).

name: The Lady's Gift
text: As the Damsel prays solemnly to the Lady of the Lake, silvery light emanates from her most ardent followers, knitting their wounds and gilding their armour.

name: Burning Gaze
text: Beams of cleansing white light flow from the Damsel's eyes, searing all traces of evil from her sight.

name: The Lady's Wrath
text: Enraged to see the lands of fair Bretonnia invaded by enemies, the Damsel invokes the wrath of the Lady of the Lake, strengthening the sword arms of her companions.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Maelstrom of Chaos
text: The Sorcerer intones words of the Dark Tongue, summoning the breath of the gods from the dark skies above and gathering it about them before unleashing it upon their enemies.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Blackened Bolts
text: With a gesture, the Sorcerer turns the tendrils of shadow that crawl about them into bolts of dark lightning.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Veil of Gloom
text: Shrouded by the lingering darkness of the Shadowlands, the Sorcerer summons the breath of the gods to dissipate the enemy's attacks.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Vortex of Darkness
text: A swirling tornado of screaming souls and inky-darkness rends and tears at those it passes, shredding flesh and spirit alike.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Shadowed Assailants
text: At the Sorcerer's command, the enemy's own shadows turn upon them, clawing at flesh with talons of darkness.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Crawling Mists
text: The Sorcerer manipulates the cloying fog of the Shadowlands to cloak the movements of their allies and hasten their advance.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: The Razing of Westerland
name: Chains of Darkness
text: Shadowy ichor materialises about the foe, solidifying into blackened shackles that weigh heavy upon their limbs.

name: Durthu's Wrath
text: Having witnessed untold destruction within his homeland, Durthu's rage echoes through Athel Loren and can be drawn upon by those who act in harmony with the forest.

name: Hidden Pathways
text: The forests of Athel Loren can be compelled to hurry the passage of those who act with its best interests at heart.

name: Ariel's Blessing
text: Channelling the power of the Mage Queen herself, the caster surrounds their allies in a healing golden light.

name: Spiteful Torrent
text: Swarms of Spites and forest insects erupt from the casters' staff, racing across the battlefield as a writhing mass that bites and stings all within reach.

name: Fury of Athel Loren
text: The caster summons forth writhing vines and briars from the ground to slash and strangle those who seek to harm Athel Loren and its inhabitants.

name: Sapping Blight
text: As roots draw water from the earth, so too can the mages of Athel Loren sap the lifeforce from their foes.

name: Swirling Mists
text: The beguiling mist that has turned about many travellers can be called upon to shroud the forces of Athel Loren from prying eyes.

name: Big Smartz
text: Gesturing lazily with a crooked staff, the caster enhances and sharpens the minds of those nearby, giving them a clearer look upon the world.

name: Acidic Bile
text: Spitting a guttural incantation, the caster launches a ball of sickly green and yellow Troll vomit toward the enemy, scorching flesh and dissolving armour.

name: Troll Brainz
text: Under the beguiling influence of this crude magic, even the most experienced warriors can quickly find themselves hopelessly confused and at a loss for what to do.

name: Ravenous Recourse
text: Through arcane means or by simply being reminded how hungry they are, the assembled Trolls race forward with a sudden burst of vigour.

name: Foetid Whirlpool
text: A deluge of foul swamp water and unmentionable waste is spun into a swirling tornado and unleashed onto the battlefield.

name: Torrent of Filth
text: With an almighty belch, the caster heaves a stream of burning bile across their foes as the battlelines clash.

name: Rapid Regeneration
text: Drawing on the regenerative properties of theTrolls, the caster channels that enhanced healing towards those in harm's way.

name: Vanhal's Danse Macabre
text: Filled with magical energy, the Undead jerk forwards on the attack with tireless and unnatural speed.

name: Hellish Vigour
text: If this spell is cast with a casting result of 6 or more, a single friendly unit that has the Necromantic Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Reserve Move special rule. If this spell is cast with a casting result of 10 or more, every friendly unit that has the Necromantic Undead special rule and is within the caster's Command range gains the Reserve Move special rule. This spell lasts until the end of this turn.

name: Raise Dead
text:
Place a unit of 2D3 Risen Zombies on the battlefield anywhere completely within 12" of this model, but not within 1" of any enemy models. This unit cannot declare a charge during the turn in which it was raised:
Troop Type: Regular Infantry
Base Size: 25 x 25 mm
Unit Size: 2-6
Equipment: Hand weapons
Special Rules: Necromantic Undead, Regeneration (6+), Skirmishers, The Newly Dead
Note that Risen Zombies are not worth any Victory Points.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Constellation of the Dragon
text: Blazing starlight leaps from the Wizard's fingertips, dancing and forming a simulacrum of the constellation of the Dragon Emperor above the battlefield.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Great Bastion
text: With a word of power from the Wizard, the earth cracks and buckles as it is transformed into ramparts upon which gleaming battlements burn with celestial flame.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Might of Heaven & Earth
text: Calling upon the elemental energies of earth and heaven, the Wizard and those around them become imbued with the pure power of the Yang.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Spirits of Wind & Shadow
text: Elemental spirits descend upon the foe, enveloping them in a whirlwind of flickering shapes and ghostly gales.As they try to advance, those afflicted find themselves waylaid by the grasping talons of unseen spirits and buffeted by spectral winds.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Accursed Mirror
text: The Wizard creates a magical barrier of shifting darkness and howling wind around the enemy. This barrier snatches the enemy's missiles from the air and swallows the tips of their spears, instantly turning the lethal power of these weapons back upon their bearers.

source_citation_book: Arcane Journal: Armies of Grand Cathay
name: Ancestral Warriors
text: TK

name: Vanhal's Danse Macabre
text:
If this spell is cast with a casting result of 7 or more, the target friendly unit gains a +D3 modifier to one of the following characteristics (to a maximum of 10). If this spell is cast with a casting result of 11 or more, the target friendly unit gains a +D3 modifier to two of the following characteristics (to a maximum of 10). This spell lasts until your next Start of Turn sub-phase.
MovementWeapon SkillInitiative
